refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation link availability at or above the customer-defined target over the agreed evaluation period.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR to explicitly state the performance metric (link availability) and the evaluation period clarifies the requirement for design and verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure link uptime statistics over the evaluation period and compare against the customer-defined availability target.
sub_frs: FR1: Ensure reliable network link connectivity for teleoperation.
FR2: Monitor and report link status continuously.
FR3: Implement failover or recovery mechanisms to maintain availability.

candidate_dps: DP1: Use redundant network links with automatic failover.
DP2: Implement real-time link health monitoring and alerting.
DP3: Design robust communication protocols with error correction and retransmission.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Use redundant network links with automatic failover.

coupling_check.notes: Redundant network links logically decouple failure modes and reduce coupling between link failures and teleoperation control.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of multiple network paths or technologies.
Risk that failover switching latency may impact teleoperation responsiveness.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating DP2 and DP3 in subsequent refinement levels to enhance monitoring and protocol robustness.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for teleoperation control, including network link management and failover.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and logical network connections used for teleoperation data exchange.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Status and health data of the teleoperation link sent to NGHE Solution and other modules.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Commands and control inputs received from NGHE Solution or operator.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment of link availability logic; low diffusion risk as failover logic is centralized.
Contains overall system coordination but less direct control over link availability.
Limited containment; vehicle hardware interfaces with network but does not manage link availability.
Minimal containment; safety alerts may depend on link status but do not manage availability.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing link availability management in the Teleoperation module minimizes diffusion and improves maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential single point of failure if Teleoperation module fails to manage failover correctly.
Dependency on external network infrastructure reliability.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Review failover latency and recovery mechanisms within Teleoperation module to mitigate risks.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Selected DP (redundant network links with failover) directly addresses the refined FR sub-functions and meets availability targets.

diffusion_risks: Low diffusion risk as link availability logic is centralized in Teleoperation module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores support Teleoperation as the best ownership candidate.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by monitoring link availability metrics and failover events within the Teleoperation module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of link availability responsibility to Teleoperation module.

recommended_revisions: Consider adding monitoring and alerting DP and IM in next refinement level.
Evaluate failover latency impact on teleoperation control responsiveness.

decision.justification: While the current decomposition and assignments are sound, integrating monitoring and protocol robustness DPs and IMs will strengthen the solution and address residual risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Ensure reliable network link connectivity for teleoperation.
FR2: Monitor and report link status continuously.
FR3: Implement failover or recovery mechanisms to maintain availability.

revise.why: To incorporate additional design parameters for monitoring and communication protocol robustness that complement the selected DP.

assumptions: Multiple network paths are available for redundancy.
Failover mechanisms can be implemented within Teleoperation module.
open_risks: Failover latency impact on teleoperation responsiveness.
evidence_needed: Performance data on failover switching times.
Network reliability statistics from operational environment.
